title: Senator Shannon Grove ( R-Bakersfield ) is pleased to announce her bipartisan legislation, Senate Bill 1043, the Accountability in Children’s Treatment (ACT) measure, has unanimous

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Accountability in Children's Treatment measure proposes greater transparency for children living in facilities licensed by the California Department of Social Services.",
    "The act requires public accessibility to information related to the use of restraints and seclusion rooms in treatment facilities.",
    "Amendments to the act will require investigations each time a restraint or seclusion room is used at a Short-Term Residential Therapeutic Program.",
    "The bill requires The Office of the Foster Care Ombudsperson to advocate for children who are restrained or secluded in these facilities.",
    "The Department of Social Services will be required to publish data on STRTPs' use of restraints or seclusion rooms every six months.",
    "Numerous reports indicate that children sent to out-of-state facilities have been abused.",
    "California previously aimed to send children to out-of-state facilities for urgent treatment needs.",
    "The Child's Continuum Pilot Program was created to develop care for foster youth after the out-of-state abuses were reported.",
    "Short Term Residential Therapeutic Programs serve as an alternative to out-of-state treatment facilities, despite still using seclusion rooms and restraints on minors."
  ]
}
```
